title: Subroutine Squirrels
short_description: Teach the squirrel one little trick, give it a name, then CALL it again and again to reach the acorn stash. Fewer blocks, more points — that's the power of functions.
description: Sandy the squirrel has a long, repetitive path to the acorn stash. Instead of tapping the same moves over and over, you define a reusable trick inside the function f() once, then drop CALL f() blocks into the main program to run it as many times as you need. It's real computer-science thinking one step beyond loops: abstraction — bundling a sequence of steps under a name and reusing it. Hit Run and watch Sandy walk your program, debugging when she bonks a wall. Grade bands ramp from a two-move function called a couple of times, to longer tricks with turns, to bigger maps that mix a function with a few extra main-program moves. Shorter, tidier programs earn a big brevity bonus. A free online functions, procedures and algorithms coding game for kids in grades 1–5, feeding your score and GP on OnlyGrins.
controls: Mouse / tap arrows to fill the function and main program, add CALL f() blocks, then Run.
